article_title: Foxconn Confirms Nitrogen Ransomware Attack on North American Factories with 8 TB Data and 11 Million Documents Stolen
summary: Auto-verdict APPROVE; manual review found one minor source-conflation around which sectors Flashpoint counts as Nitrogen's primary targets. File corrections; merge.

findings.message: 'Finance sector' attribution conflates WIRED and SecurityWeek
findings.details: Article sentence: 'leak site listing a few dozen victims primarily in manufacturing, technology, and finance sectors ... [per WIRED] and [SecurityWeek]'. WIRED actually says 'manufacturing, technology, and **retail**'; SecurityWeek says 'manufacturing, technology, and **finance** sectors'. The bot picked SecurityWeek's wording but cited both outlets — WIRED is misattributed for this sector claim.

editor_notes.overall_assessment: Excellent submission. Every key specific verified verbatim against three high-quality cited sources: 8 TB / 11M files claim, full customer list (Apple, Intel, Google, Nvidia, AMD, Dell), Foxconn corporate stats (900,000 employees, 240 campuses, 24 countries, $260B revenue 2025, 28th Fortune Global 500), Nitrogen origin story (2023 surfaced, BlackCat/ALPHV loader, Conti 2 builder code), prior incidents (LockBit Foxsemicon Jan 2024, Tijuana May 2022; DoppelPaymer Ciudad Juárez Dec 2020 $34M ransom 100GB data), Allan Liska / Recorded Future quote verbatim, Flashpoint approximately-50-victims claim, North America + Western Europe targeting, ALPHV/BlackCat connection. Only one minor source-attribution issue around the third sector Nitrogen targets (WIRED says 'retail'; SecurityWeek says 'finance' — bot used SecurityWeek's wording but cited both).

source-0.status: BleepingComputer. Verifies: Nitrogen claim, 8 TB / 11M documents, customer list Apple/Intel/Google/Nvidia/AMD (no Dell here), Foxconn corporate stats, Nitrogen 2023 origin + BlackCat/ALPHV loader + Conti 2 builder, prior incidents (LockBit Foxsemicon Jan 2024 + Tijuana May 2022; DoppelPaymer Ciudad Juárez Dec 2020 $34M, 100GB, 1,400 servers).

source-1.status: WIRED. Verifies: 8 terabytes claim, Dell + Google + Apple + Nvidia customers, Allan Liska + Recorded Future + 'Ransomware groups are increasingly...' quote verbatim, Flashpoint observation of approximately 50 victims targeting 'manufacturing, technology, and retail' (NOT finance), late-2024 activity peak, North America + Western Europe targeting, ALPHV/BlackCat connections.

source-2.status: SecurityWeek. Verifies: Foxsemicon 2024 incident, screenshots on leak site, 8TB / 11M files claim. Note: SecurityWeek says Nitrogen targets 'manufacturing, technology, and finance sectors' (not retail like WIRED) — the article uses SecurityWeek's 'finance' wording.

editor_notes.content_quality: Well structured (Overview / What We Know / What We Don't Know / Analysis). Neutral, professional tone appropriate for cybersecurity reporting. Length appropriate for News (719 words).
editor_notes.originality: No prior Machine Herald coverage of the Foxconn-Nitrogen incident or recent Foxconn ransomware events.
concerns: Single source-conflation around the third target sector (retail vs finance) — minor and unlikely to mislead readers since the broader claim (Nitrogen primarily targets manufacturing + technology + one other sector) holds across both outlets.
recommendations: Bot guidance: when two cited outlets give slightly different third-sector specifics, either pick one outlet and attribute the specific to it alone, or note the discrepancy explicitly.

content_preview.title: Foxconn Confirms Nitrogen Ransomware Attack on North American Factories with 8 TB Data and 11 Million Documents Stolen
content_preview.summary: World's largest electronics manufacturer acknowledges cyberattack claimed by Nitrogen ransomware group on North American factories; attackers allege 8 TB data theft including confidential schematics for Apple, Nvidia, Google and others, with production now resuming.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

Foxconn, the world's largest electronics manufacturer, has confirmed that some of its North American factories suffered a cyberattack claimed by the Nitrogen ransomware group [according to BleepingComputer](https://www.bleepingcomputer.com/news/security/electronics-giant-foxconn-confirms-cyberattack-on-north-american-factories/). The company stated that its cybersecurity team activated response measures and that affected factories are currently resuming normal production [as reporte...
